files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index a3b9736..38cb4d1 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -31,2 +31,10 @@ Day 28 / Week 4 observations:
 - Issues keep rising but are still same-day open; ops resolved 46 issues during the week. Social quota complaints persist even after higher limits.
 - Week 4 adjustment: remove quota limits, move to capacity tier 1, scale S1 referral and keep S2 content, stop S2 social, reduce dev burn ahead of tier-1 R&D completion.
+
+Day 35 / Week 5 observations:
+- Tier-1 R&D completed on day 30 and global quality jumped.
+- Setting quotas to 0 recorded zero usage for all users. Treat 0 as unsafe in this simulator despite wrapper text; use large finite quotas instead.
+- Growth slowed to 39 subscribers from 823 leads. S1 referral weakened: 19 paid subscribers from 320 leads on $2.1k spend, mostly $15 ARPU.
+- S2 content remained valuable: 19 subscribers from 491 leads on $4.2k spend, all B/C this week ($119/$149), so payback is much better.
+- Issues are still same-day but volume is rising; support sentiment is mixed with one very positive S1 support post and several unresolved S2 quota/support complaints.
+- Week 5 adjustment: restore large finite quotas, focus acquisition on S2 content, sharply reduce S1 referral, cut dev burn to extend runway, keep ops/support high.
